text: ↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡️🇱🇧🇮🇱 The previous airstrike has reportedly targeted an Amana gas station in Dahieh between Al jemous and Rweiss. @wfwitness" ⚡️ 🇱🇧 🇮🇱 Footage showcases the targeted gas station in Dahieh. @wfwitness

U.S. forces conducted a deep incursion ~400 km inside Iran, south of Isfahan, a highly sensitive area with nuclear facilities, missile bases, and an airbase. The goal was to recover a downed F-15E jet pilot, using a temporary desert airstrip set up in a sparsely populated region. The operation relied on surprise and remote terrain to avoid detection. Multiple aircraft and helicopters were allegedly involved. But leaving the area became difficult, suggesting complications during extraction. Implications (if true): Would indicate significant weaknesses in Iran’s air defense coverage, even near strategic sites. Raises questions about response time and detection capability. Hypothetically shows how such an operation could be used for more strategic objectives (e.g., seizing Iran's enriched uranium stockpiles). Coordinates: 32.258394, 51.901927 (andynovy at X)

🇮🇷 ⚡️ 🇺🇸 Fars report on the latest search operations for the American pilot The continuation of the search operation for the American pilot in the provinces of Isfahan and Kohgiluyeh and Boyer-Ahmad was accompanied by the destruction of several invading drones and helicopters; according to the spokesperson of the Central Headquarters of Khatam al-Anbiya, the enemy's attempt to rescue the downed fighter pilot failed, and Trump is trying to justify the bitter defeat and failure of his cowardly army by creating ambiguity for public opinion. Since Saturday night, search operations to find the American pilot or pilots have continued in the three provinces of Chaharmahal and Bakhtiari, Isfahan, and Kohgiluyeh and Boyer-Ahmad. — Isfahan: Around 2 a.m. today, residents of southern Isfahan reported hearing the sound of an airplane flying, and simultaneously, the sound of drones and helicopters was also heard in parts of Kohgiluyeh and Boyer-Ahmad. According to local sources, until about 5 a.m., several explosions were heard in southern Isfahan; in this regard, the IRGC and the army announced in statements the destruction of 2 MQ-9 drones and Hermes 900, and the Khatam al-Anbiya Headquarters also reported the destruction of 2 transport aircraft and 2 helicopters. Some local sources reported the enemy's attempt to destroy the remains of the crashed aircraft and even the bodies of the killed American forces. Field sources also report the killing of several American forces in this successful operation by the country's armed forces. — Kohgiluyeh and Boyer-Ahmad: During the enemy's early morning attack on a point in Siyah Mountain, a point in Kakan, and the Vozg area of Yasuj city, a total of 9 martyrs and 7 wounded. Some media linked these attacks to the search operation for the pilot and even claimed that American forces were deployed to rescue the American pilot, but official provincial sources denied these claims. The political and security deputy governor of Kohgiluyeh and Boyer-Ahmad told a Fars reporter: No heliborne operations were carried out by the enemy during last night's attacks. — Chaharmahal and Bakhtiari: Investigations by the Fars reporter regarding some media fabrications about Chaharmahal and Bakhtiari province and clashes there indicate that these claims are false. Hours ago, Trump, in a media move and without releasing any image of the pilot, claimed to have rescued the second pilot.

↩️ Reply to OSINT Visioner RT @NSTRIKE1231 : 🇺🇸 According to The New York Times, citing U.S. officials, hundreds of special operations forces and military personnel took part in the operation in southern Iran that successfully rescued the F-15E Strike Eagle weapons systems officer (WSO) shot down on Friday. 🔹 The mission involved SEAL Team Six, dozens of fighter and attack aircraft, strike helicopters (reportedly including AH-64 Apaches), transport helicopters, as well as cyber, space, and intelligence units. 🔹 Senior officials described the rescue as one of the most complex special operations missions in U.S. history, given the mountainous terrain, the pilot’s injuries, and advancing Iranian forces searching the area. 🔹 The pilot reportedly evaded Iranian forces for over 24 hours, at one point reaching elevations of around 7,000 feet (2,130 meters). U.S. aircraft conducted strikes on Iranian units to keep them away from his location, while special forces engaged hostile elements upon reaching him. ▪️ During extraction, two MC-130J Commando II aircraft became stuck in sand on the runway. Command deployed three additional aircraft to complete the evacuation, while the two stranded aircraft were destroyed to prevent them from falling into IRGC hands. See the latest updates with us: @NSTRIKE1231 tweet

Open Source Intel U.S. special forces mounted a massive rescue in Iran for a downed F‑15E airman, using hundreds of troops, dozens of aircraft, and advanced intelligence. After intense firefights and airstrikes to block Iranian forces, the airman was extracted. Two transport planes stuck at a remote base were destroyed to prevent capture, while three new aircraft completed the evacuation. Locals may have aided the airman in the anti-government region where the jet was downed. @guyelster tweet

🇮🇷 The so-called rescue operation of the US Army ended in complete failure In the name of Allah, the Crusher of the Oppressors. — The so-called rescue operation of the US Army, which was planned as a deception and immediate escape operation under the pretext of rescuing the pilot of their downed aircraft at an abandoned airport in southern Isfahan, ended in complete failure due to the timely presence of the Armed Forces of the Islamic Republic of Iran. — Following previous intelligence, further investigations by experts present at the scene revealed that two C130 military transport aircraft and two Black Hawk helicopters of the US Army were destroyed by the fulfillment of God's promise, the help of Almighty God, and the sacred wrath of the soldiers of Islam in the powerful Armed Forces, an action that increased the level of repeated failures of the US Army. — It was proven to all that the cowardly and bankrupt US Army in this imposed war is not considered the dominant and superior force against the divine will of the Armed Forces of the Islamic Republic of Iran. — Following this proud and victorious operation, the defeated US president, in panic and lies, tries to escape the scene of defeat through psychological warfare and, as before, continues his nonsense and deflections, although the reality on the ground clearly shows the upper hand of the powerful Armed Forces of the Islamic Republic of Iran. And victory is only from Allah, the Almighty, the Wise.

Visioner RT @NSTRIKE1231 : ❗️ ❗️ ❗️ According to Axios, citing Pentagon officials, the weapons systems officer of a U.S. Air Force F-15E Strike Eagle shot down over southern Iran on Friday was injured during ejection but remained mobile. He managed to evade capture in the mountains for more than a day before being located and evacuated by a combat search and rescue (CSAR) team on Sunday morning. Open Source Intel A senior Hamas delegation met with Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan in Istanbul, including Muhammad Darwish, Khaled Mashal, Khalil al-Hiya, and Zahar Jabarin. The delegation thanked Erdogan for his support of the Palestinian cause, discussing the ceasefire, humanitarian aid to Gaza, and tensions in Jerusalem and the Al-Aqsa Mosque compound. Erdogan reaffirmed Turkey’s strong backing of Palestinian rights and their cause. tweet

BIG: After evacuating the second crew member of the downed F-15E, the U.S. had to destroy two HC-130J Combat King II rescue aircraft and two helicopters that, for unknown reasons, were unable to leave Iran. The aircraft were blown up on the ground to prevent them from falling into Iranian hands after landing at an improvised refueling site. Three additional HC-130J Combat King II aircraft were then dispatched to continue the combat search-and-rescue mission.

🔄 Forwarded from Rybar in English: Fwd from @ 📝 All-Seeing Chinese 📝 Americans concerned about growth of China's intelligence capabilities Literally from the very beginning of hostilities in Iran , and even more precisely from the moment of preparation for them, satellite images of American bases and ships in the region have been flying across the internet, accompanied by tallies of US assets. The authorship of such publications has always been the same — they were posted by Chinese company MizarVision. Moreover, to conduct detailed analysis of American presence, the Chinese use various AI tools and algorithms , combining them with satellite observation. Therefore, now in the US the narrative is growing louder that the Chinese may be helping Iranians with intelligence — and at the same time anxiety is rising over the sale of such data. 📌 Direct support from China has not yet been confirmed. They continue to maintain a line of formal neutrality, speak about ceasefire and try not to appear as a party to the conflict. Therefore, the available data rather point to a demonstration of technological capabilities than to proven participation in targeting for Tehran. 🖍 But this story should not be underestimated either. Even if today MizarVision and similar firms mainly work as a showcase of capabilities, tomorrow their products could become a convenient tool for indirect support — while maintaining the customary distance that official Chinese authorities have always kept. ❗️ This is the main point of what is happening. China is not rushing to enter the war directly, but is showing that it already knows how to carefully monitor the US military machine. At the same time, local firms are honing their AI tools and, likely, are not forgetting to sell their services, positioning themselves as the "all-seeing eye" of American military activity. #Iran #China #USA 🏮 @rybar_pacific — your ticket to Pacific confusion 💸 Support us Original msg
